metadata.title: ExampleCo Medium Corridor Assessment Results
metadata.remarks: Synthetic assessment-results style output derived from the ExampleCo public proof bundle.

results.title: ExampleCo medium corridor result
results.description: Result set derived from the public ExampleCo proof bundle.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-101
observations.description: The synthetic identity export shows MFA required and conditional access enabled for scoped administrative identities.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-128
observations.description: The identity export shows named accounts required for the scoped infrastructure access plane and no shared administrative accounts present.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-129
observations.description: The architecture export shows customer boundaries enforced, production separated from non-production, and no undeclared cross-environment paths in scope.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-130
observations.description: The identity export shows a minimum password length of at least 12 characters, no maximum-length restriction, and common-password blocking enabled for the scoped accounts.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-131
observations.description: The edge export shows a managed web application firewall attached to the scoped public ingress path, running in blocking mode, with the managed rule set active.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-102
observations.description: The cloud logging export shows logging enabled and retention declared for the scoped runtime.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-132
observations.description: The monitoring export shows the scoped assets forwarding telemetry into the declared central sink with detection rules enabled.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-103
observations.description: The ingress export states HTTPS-only listeners, managed certificates, and a minimum TLS version of 1.2.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-124
observations.description: The network boundary export shows the declared ingress path traverses the managed default-deny boundary.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-125
observations.description: The network boundary export shows admin ingress restricted to declared source ranges with no unapproved admin exposure.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-126
observations.description: The ingress export shows HTTPS-only listeners and an explicit plaintext-disabled transport policy.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-127
observations.description: The storage export shows encryption enabled across the scoped customer data stores and no unencrypted store present in the declared slice.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-104
observations.description: The IAM export states no user-managed keys are present for the scoped runtime service accounts.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-105
observations.description: The asset inventory export shows observed regions match the declared approved region list and no undeclared regions are present.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-106
observations.description: The backup configuration export shows snapshots enabled with a 24-hour schedule and a declared immutable window.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-107
observations.description: The training claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named security manager.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-108
observations.description: The restore exercise claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the platform lead.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-109
observations.description: The vendor register claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the privacy manager.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-110
observations.description: The DSR runbook claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the privacy manager.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-112
observations.description: The corridor expects a typed access-review export, but no such evidence is attached to this public synthetic pack.
observations.remarks: EX-CLAIM-112 is blocked because no typed evidence is attached.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-113
observations.description: The repository export shows the default branch is protected, approvals are required, status checks are enforced, and force pushes are blocked.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-114
observations.description: The CI export shows workflow review, trusted publishing, protected refs, and environment approvals are all required.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-115
observations.description: The change-review governance claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named change owner.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-116
observations.description: The access-review closure claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named identity governance owner.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-133
observations.description: The monitoring-operations claim is documentary and signed by the named detection engineering owner.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-117
observations.description: The configuration-exception claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named platform security owner.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-118
observations.description: The patch-exception claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named operations owner.

observations.title: Observation for EX-CLAIM-120
observations.description: The incident-runbook claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named security operations owner.

observations.description: The incident-contact claim is a signed synthetic attestation by the named security operations owner.
